title: useWindowScroll
description: Tracks the scroll position of the window and updates in real-time.
summary: Monitors and provides current window scroll values.

body.raw:
## Usage

The `useWindowScroll` hook allows you to track the scroll position of the window. This can be useful for features like infinite scrolling, sticky elements, or scroll-based animations. Here’s an example to demonstrate how to use the `useWindowScroll` hook to monitor the scroll position of the window.

<UseWindowScrollDemosUsage />

---

## Source Codes

Full working code example, including necessary markup and styles.
You can copy and paste this code directly to start using the component immediately.

<SourceCodes name="use-window-scroll" />

`,(0,n.jsx)(r,{name:"use-window-scroll",code:`"use client";\r
import { useEffect, useState } from "react";\r
import { useWindowEvent } from "@/hooks/use-window-event";\r
\r
interface ScrollPosition {\r
  x: number;\r
  y: number;\r
}\r
\r
function getScrollPosition(): ScrollPosition {\r
  return typeof window !== "undefined" ? { x: window.pageXOffset, y: window.pageYOffset } : { x: 0, y: 0 };\r
}\r
\r
function scrollTo({ x, y }: Partial<ScrollPosition>) {\r
  if (typeof window !== "undefined") {\r
    const scrollOptions: ScrollToOptions = { behavior: "smooth" };\r
\r
    if (typeof x === "number") {\r
      scrollOptions.left = x;\r
    }\r
\r
    if (typeof y === "number") {\r
      scrollOptions.top = y;\r
    }\r
\r
    window.scrollTo(scrollOptions);\r
  }\r
}\r
\r
function checkIfBottom(): boolean {\r
  const windowHeight = window.innerHeight;\r
  const documentHeight = document.documentElement.scrollHeight;\r
  const scrollTop = window.scrollY;\r
  const tolerance = 5; // small tolerance\r
\r
  return scrollTop + windowHeight + tolerance >= documentHeight;\r
}\r
\r
export function useWindowScroll() {\r
  const [bottom, setBottom] = useState(false);\r
  const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false);\r
  const [position, setPosition] = useState<ScrollPosition>({ x: 0, y: 0 });\r
  const [isScroll, setIsScroll] = useState(false);\r
\r
  let scrollTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;\r
\r
  const handleScroll = () => {\r
    const pos = getScrollPosition();\r
    setPosition(pos);\r
    setBottom(checkIfBottom());\r
    setIsScroll(true);\r
\r
    if (scrollTimeout) {\r
      clearTimeout(scrollTimeout);\r
    }\r
\r
    scrollTimeout = setTimeout(() => {\r
      setIsScroll(false);\r
    }, 1500);\r
  };\r
\r
  useWindowEvent("scroll", handleScroll);\r
  useWindowEvent("resize", handleScroll);\r
\r
  useEffect(() => {\r
    handleScroll();\r
    setMounted(true);\r
  }, []);\r
\r
  const scrollToBottom = () => {\r
    window.scrollTo({\r
      top: document.documentElement.scrollHeight,\r
      behavior: "smooth"\r
    });\r
  };\r
\r
  const scrollToTop = () => {\r
    window.scrollTo({\r
      top: 0,\r
      behavior: "smooth"\r
    });\r
  };\r
\r
  const scrollWindow = bottom ? scrollToTop : scrollToBottom;\r
\r
  return { position, scrollTo, bottom, mounted, isScroll, scrollWindow };\r
}`,ext:".ts",repo:"web/hooks/use-window-scroll.ts"})]})}function h(e={}){let{wrapper:o}=e.components||{};return o?(0,n.jsx)(o,{...e,children:(0,n.jsx)(d,{...e})}):d(e)}function u(e,o){throw new Error("Expected "+(o?"component":"object")+" `"+e+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return v(T);})();
